title: Steirachne deccanensis
taxonomy: Steirachne deccanensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. It belongs to the genus Steirachne, which consists of a small group of grasses found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Steirachne deccanensis is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Monocots, order Poales, family Poaceae, genus Steirachne, and species S. deccanensis. This classification indicates that the plant is a monocot, a member of the grass family, and a specific species within the Steirachne genus. Further research may be necessary to confirm the accuracy of the classification.

intro: Steirachne deccanensis is a species of plant that belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as a type of grass or grama grass. The plant is traditionally valued for its medicinal properties, and it is believed to have health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in treating various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Steirachne deccanensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
